data.narrative:
Umbra is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by David Langan and Gabe Dominocielo that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 5 public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with direct peer comparables drawn from 3 same-stage, same-sector businesses: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $100M capital raised proxy, yielding $190M. The peer-median approach uses the three identified comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $400M with a range from $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because Umbra's revenue is not disclosed. The public Space & Aerospace median trades at 59.8x sales across six tickers, but without revenue visibility, this method remains inoperable.

The two applicable methods support a valuation range of $162M to $460M, with a triangulated median of $295M. The wide dispersion—driven by Aalyria's outlier $1.3B valuation—reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set. Key uncertainties include Umbra's revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether its technology or market positioning justifies valuation toward the upper or lower quartile of the peer range.
